in-depth description of twerking

Lake Vostok sits under four kilometers of Antarctic ice, sealed from the surface for approximately 15 million years. When scientists drilled into it in 2012, the water samples contained bacterial DNA matching no known species on Earth. The full analysis of those samples has not been publicly released.
the best way to stir your coffee with no spoon

In 1908 an explosion over Tunguska Siberia flattened 2,000 square kilometers of forest in a radial pattern consistent with an aerial detonation. No crater was ever found. No meteorite fragments were recovered. The estimated force was 1,000 times the atomic bomb dropped on Hiroshima. What caused it has never been definitively established.
comparing 2 items that look identical but one is made of cake

Skinwalker Ranch in Utah has documented reports from law enforcement and scientists of cattle mutilations with surgical precision, objects moving against gravity, and electromagnetic anomalies that disabled equipment. In 1996 the US government purchased adjacent land through a front company. The purpose of that purchase has never been officially acknowledged.

The Dyatlov Pass investigation was officially closed three times. Each time new evidence caused it to reopen. In 2019 the Russian government issued a final conclusion and declared the case permanently closed. Three of the original investigators publicly stated they do not accept it.
unusual animal friends and their adorable adventures

Kowloon Walled City housed 33,000 people in a single city block with no sunlight on the lower floors. Longtime residents described a layout that changed between visits. When it was demolished in 1993 the architectural survey found 17 rooms that appeared on no resident's mental map of the building.
Incredible easter egg hiding spots

a short story about lisa o'connel, a clinically delusional alcoholic who had nothing to live for except drinking wine, covering up the deaths of immigrant employees, getting drunk at staff parties and slapping people, inserting herself into matters that have nothing to do with her, she is literally shaped like a pair and has gray hair that looks like a wig made of grass, she thinks she's the main character of her own film noir movie, but in reality she's a washed up loser who lives in bracebridge ontario, her one hope in life was shattered when she thought a new manager at the hotel named michael johnstone might be a love interest, because he was also very fat and delusional and utterly terrible at leadership and couldn't even help staff pick up garbage because he couldn't barely reach his own knees, unfortunately, lisa's thoughts that michael would be her love interest were shattered when it turned out that michael was actually a homosexual predator who fled the premises and abandoned his position without notice when he was caught attempting to match with a staff member on grindr.
my mother in law is a skinwalker

The Russian Sleep Experiment is a horror story. It is fiction. The reason it circulates as fact is that real Soviet sleep deprivation research from the same era remains classified to this day. The story feels true because something true is hidden underneath it.

When the USS Scorpion sank in 1968, the Navy heard every second of it through a classified hydrophone network. They tracked the implosion sounds for several minutes. The full recording has never been released to the families of the 99 men aboard.
Shadow work reframes emotional triggers as information rather than interference — a disproportionate reaction almost always means something older than today is responding.
alien sightings in a small northern Ontario city

Unit 731: Japan's Doctors Unpunished (36 chars) Unit 731 was a secret Imperial Japanese military unit that conducted live experiments on thousands of prisoners during World War II including deliberate infection with plague and cholera. After Japan's surrender the US government granted full immunity to every member of Unit 731 in exchange for the experimental data. Not one person was ever tried for war crimes.

In 1518 in Strasbourg France hundreds of people danced uncontrollably in the streets for weeks until dozens died from exhaustion. City officials responded by hiring musicians and building a public stage to help them keep dancing. No medical cause has ever been identified.

A 1980 declassified military document describes soldiers kept in a room with no windows, no clocks, and no time cues for 90 days. By day 75 several reported that time had stopped moving and started pooling. The experiment was terminated before day 90. The reason is redacted in the public version.
